# FORGE Rugby Coach Review Sample Pack

**Generated**: 2026-06-23  
**Generator**: `scripts/generate_coach_review_samples.py`  
**Purpose**: External S&C coach review — 48 samples across 8 roles × 3 levels

## How to Use This Pack

1. Review the **Coverage Matrix** below to understand the scope.
2. Each **role × level** section contains two samples (A and B).
3. Each sample includes: athlete profile, rationale, full program, exposure summary.
4. At the end of each sample, use the **Coach Review Prompts** to guide your feedback.
5. Record your overall feedback in the separate **Review Sheet** (`*_review_sheet.md`).

| **Session Duration** | 60 min |
| **Season Phase** | Off Season |

| **Credibility Score** | 1.0 |

### Why This Sample Exists

- Represents a **beginner Prop** athlete in the **rugby** pathway.
- Training age of 0.5 year(s) with limited gym history.
- Goal emphasis: **Strength** — drives blueprint selection and session architecture.
- Competition context: **Off Season** — affects volume, intensity, and conditioning density.
- Equipment access: **Basic Gym** — constrains exercise selection and loading options.
- Variant **A** of two — differs in goal emphasis, training load, or seasonal context to show FORGE's range within the same archetype.

### Role Profile / Bias Summary

- Prop role -> maximal force / upper-body emphasis / collision robustness bias
- Sprint exposure moderated per role
- Jump/landing volume capped per role
- Deceleration accumulation capped per role
- Rotational exposure moderated per role
- High-eccentric tolerance allowed per role

-   Force emphasis:           0.9
-   Velocity emphasis:        0.2

rugby Athlete (strength)
==================================================
Level: Beginner | 2x/week | 8 weeks

Personalization Notes:

  Prop role -> maximal force / upper-body emphasis / collision robustness bias

WARMUP
------------------------------
  Raise (3 min)
    - Forward jog (2 min)
      Cue: Light feet, tall posture
    - Skipping (basic) (1 min)
      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (4 min)
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Band YTWs (1 min)
      Cue: Squeeze shoulder blades, keep ribs down
    - Pass-through (PVC) (0 min)
      Cue: Keep arms straight, go overhead and back
    - Dead bug (0 min)
      Cue: Press low back into floor, move opposite limbs
    - Cossack squat (1 min)
      Cue: Shift weight fully to bent leg, keep heel down
  Potentiate (2 min)
    - Sub-max bench (50-60%) (1 min)
      Cue: Explosive concentric, controlled descent
    - Sub-max squat (50-60%) (1 min)
      Cue: Stand fast, controlled descent

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.YOUTH_FOUNDATION — Build movement literacy, general strength, and athleticism. No specialisation. Fun + competence.
- **Best phase**: off_season, pre_season, in_season, transition | **Best training age**: 0-2 years
- **Role week note**: Prop role -> maximal force / upper-body emphasis / collision robustness bias
- **Role week note**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: Jump/landing volume capped per role
- **Role week note**: Deceleration accumulation capped per role
- **Role week note**: Rotational exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: High-eccentric tolerance allowed per role
- **Personalization**: Prop role -> maximal force & collision robustness emphasis
- **Personalization**: Prop role -> maximal force / upper-body emphasis / collision robustness bias
- **Personalization**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Personalization**: Jump/landing volume capped per role
- **Rationale**: Blueprint: Youth Foundation (U14-U20) (ID: 7)
- **Rationale**: Goal: strength | Level: Beginner
- **Rationale**: Role-aware planning applied: prop
- **Rationale**: Block: 8 weeks, 2x/week — 16 total sessions

- Equipment access: **Basic Gym** — constrains exercise selection and loading options.
- Variant **B** of two — differs in goal emphasis, training load, or seasonal context to show FORGE's range within the same archetype.

rugby Athlete (mass)
==================================================
Level: Beginner | 3x/week | 8 weeks
Goal: mass | Equipment: Basic Gym

Personalization Notes:
  Prop role -> maximal force & collision robustness emphasis
  Prop role -> maximal force / upper-body emphasis / collision robustness bias

  Jump/landing volume capped per role
  Deceleration accumulation capped per role
  Rotational exposure moderated per role

WARMUP
------------------------------
  Raise (3 min)
    - Forward jog (2 min)
      Cue: Light feet, tall posture
    - Skipping (basic) (1 min)
      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (4 min)
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Band YTWs (1 min)
      Cue: Squeeze shoulder blades, keep ribs down
    - Pass-through (PVC) (0 min)
      Cue: Keep arms straight, go overhead and back
    - Dead bug (0 min)
      Cue: Press low back into floor, move opposite limbs
    - Cossack squat (1 min)
      Cue: Shift weight fully to bent leg, keep heel down
  Potentiate (2 min)
    - Sub-max bench (50-60%) (1 min)
      Cue: Explosive concentric, controlled descent
    - Sub-max squat (50-60%) (1 min)
      Cue: Stand fast, controlled descent

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.FULL_BODY_STRENGTH — General strength accrual across all major movement patterns. Highest frequency of each pattern per week.
- **Best phase**: off_season, pre_season | **Best training age**: 0-3 years
- **Role week note**: Prop role -> maximal force / upper-body emphasis / collision robustness bias
- **Role week note**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: Jump/landing volume capped per role
- **Role week note**: Deceleration accumulation capped per role
- **Role week note**: Rotational exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: High-eccentric tolerance allowed per role
- **Personalization**: Prop role -> maximal force & collision robustness emphasis
- **Personalization**: Prop role -> maximal force / upper-body emphasis / collision robustness bias
- **Personalization**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Personalization**: Jump/landing volume capped per role
- **Rationale**: Blueprint: Full Body Strength (ID: 1)
- **Rationale**: Goal: mass | Level: Beginner
- **Rationale**: Role-aware planning applied: prop
- **Rationale**: Block: 8 weeks, 3x/week — 24 total sessions

  Activate (4 min)
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Band YTWs (1 min)
      Cue: Squeeze shoulder blades, keep ribs down
    - Pass-through (PVC) (0 min)
      Cue: Keep arms straight, go overhead and back
    - Dead bug (0 min)
      Cue: Press low back into floor, move opposite limbs
    - Cossack squat (1 min)
      Cue: Shift weight fully to bent leg, keep heel down

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.RUGBY_OFF_SEASON — Build collision-ready strength, power, and mass. Contact preparation with conditioning.
- **Best phase**: off_season | **Best training age**: 2-5 years
- **Role week note**: Prop role -> maximal force / upper-body emphasis / collision robustness bias
- **Role week note**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: Jump/landing volume capped per role
- **Role week note**: Deceleration accumulation capped per role
- **Role week note**: Rotational exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: High-eccentric tolerance allowed per role
- **Personalization**: Prop role -> maximal force & collision robustness emphasis
- **Personalization**: Prop role -> maximal force / upper-body emphasis / collision robustness bias
- **Personalization**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Personalization**: Jump/landing volume capped per role
- **Rationale**: Blueprint: Rugby Off-Season (ID: 9)
- **Rationale**: Goal: strength | Level: Intermediate
- **Rationale**: Role-aware planning applied: prop
- **Rationale**: Block: 8 weeks, 4x/week — 32 total sessions

- Represents a **intermediate Prop** athlete in the **rugby** pathway.
- Training age of 2.5 year(s) with solid gym history.
- Goal emphasis: **Mass** — drives blueprint selection and session architecture.
- Competition context: **Pre Season** — affects volume, intensity, and conditioning density.
- Equipment access: **Commercial Gym** — constrains exercise selection and loading options.
- Variant **B** of two — differs in goal emphasis, training load, or seasonal context to show FORGE's range within the same archetype.

### Full Program Output

```
rugby Athlete (mass)
==================================================
Level: Intermediate | 3x/week | 8 weeks
Goal: mass | Equipment: Commercial Gym

Personalization Notes:
  Prop role -> maximal force & collision robustness emphasis
  Prop role -> maximal force / upper-body emphasis / collision robustness bias
  Sprint exposure moderated per role
  Jump/landing volume capped per role
  Deceleration accumulation capped per role
  Rotational exposure moderated per role
  High-eccentric tolerance allowed per role

WARMUP
------------------------------
  Raise (3 min)
    - Forward jog (2 min)
      Cue: Light feet, tall posture
    - Skipping (basic) (1 min)
      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (4 min)
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Band YTWs (1 min)
      Cue: Squeeze shoulder blades, keep ribs down
    - Pass-through (PVC) (0 min)
      Cue: Keep arms straight, go overhead and back
    - Dead bug (0 min)
      Cue: Press low back into floor, move opposite limbs
    - Cossack squat (1 min)
      Cue: Shift weight fully to bent leg, keep heel down
  Potentiate (2 min)
    - Sub-max bench (50-60%) (1 min)
      Cue: Explosive concentric, controlled descent
    - Sub-max squat (50-60%) (1 min)
      Cue: Stand fast, controlled descent

--- W1 S1 (accumulation) ---
  [TEST] Movement / Technical Benchmark | Jump / Power Test
  [DLKD] DLKD
    - Goblet Squat  3 x 6-8  |  RPE 7-9, maximal force emphasis  |  rest: 4:00 min
  [DLHD] DLHD
    - RDL  3 x 6-8  |  RPE 7-9, maximal force emphasis  |  rest: 4:00 min

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.FULL_BODY_STRENGTH — General strength accrual across all major movement patterns. Highest frequency of each pattern per week.
- **Best phase**: off_season, pre_season | **Best training age**: 0-3 years
- **Role week note**: Prop role -> maximal force / upper-body emphasis / collision robustness bias
- **Role week note**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: Jump/landing volume capped per role
- **Role week note**: Deceleration accumulation capped per role
- **Role week note**: Rotational exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: High-eccentric tolerance allowed per role
- **Personalization**: Prop role -> maximal force & collision robustness emphasis
- **Personalization**: Prop role -> maximal force / upper-body emphasis / collision robustness bias
- **Personalization**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Personalization**: Jump/landing volume capped per role
- **Rationale**: Blueprint: Full Body Strength (ID: 1)
- **Rationale**: Goal: mass | Level: Intermediate
- **Rationale**: Role-aware planning applied: prop
- **Rationale**: Block: 8 weeks, 3x/week — 24 total sessions

### Coach Review Prompts

- [ ] Does this **Prop (Intermediate)** program feel role-appropriate?
- [ ] Is the weekly training frequency appropriate for this athlete archetype?
- [ ] Are exercise selections and sequencing coach-like and sport-relevant?
- [ ] Does the conditioning density match the sport's physiological demands?
- [ ] Is the loading / intensity progression realistic for this level?
- [ ] What is the single most impactful change you would make?
- [ ] Would you trust this program with a real athlete at this level?

### Why This Sample Exists

- Represents a **advanced Prop** athlete in the **rugby** pathway.
- Training age of 5.0 year(s) with extensive gym history.
- Goal emphasis: **Strength** — drives blueprint selection and session architecture.
- Competition context: **Pre Season** — affects volume, intensity, and conditioning density.
- Equipment access: **Commercial Gym** — constrains exercise selection and loading options.
- Variant **A** of two — differs in goal emphasis, training load, or seasonal context to show FORGE's range within the same archetype.

### Role Profile / Bias Summary

- Prop role -> maximal force / upper-body emphasis / collision robustness bias
- Sprint exposure moderated per role
- Jump/landing volume capped per role
- Deceleration accumulation capped per role
- Rotational exposure moderated per role
- High-eccentric tolerance allowed per role

-   Force emphasis:           0.9
-   Velocity emphasis:        0.2
-   Conditioning emphasis:    0.4
-   Rotation emphasis:        0.3
-   Landing emphasis:         0.3
-   Upper-body emphasis:      0.7
- 
-   Sprint exposure target:   Low
-   Jump/Landing exposure target: Low
-   Deceleration exposure target: Low
-   Rotation exposure target: Low
-   Conditioning density:     Moderate

rugby Athlete (strength)
==================================================
Level: Advanced | 3x/week | 8 weeks

Personalization Notes:

  Prop role -> maximal force / upper-body emphasis / collision robustness bias

WARMUP
------------------------------
  Raise (3 min)
    - Forward jog (2 min)
      Cue: Light feet, tall posture
    - Skipping (basic) (1 min)
      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (4 min)
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Band YTWs (1 min)
      Cue: Squeeze shoulder blades, keep ribs down
    - Pass-through (PVC) (0 min)
      Cue: Keep arms straight, go overhead and back
    - Dead bug (0 min)
      Cue: Press low back into floor, move opposite limbs
    - Cossack squat (1 min)
      Cue: Shift weight fully to bent leg, keep heel down
  Potentiate (2 min)
    - Sub-max bench (50-60%) (1 min)
      Cue: Explosive concentric, controlled descent
    - Sub-max squat (50-60%) (1 min)
      Cue: Stand fast, controlled descent

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.FULL_BODY_STRENGTH — General strength accrual across all major movement patterns. Highest frequency of each pattern per week.
- **Best phase**: off_season, pre_season | **Best training age**: 0-3 years
- **Role week note**: Prop role -> maximal force / upper-body emphasis / collision robustness bias
- **Role week note**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: Jump/landing volume capped per role
- **Role week note**: Deceleration accumulation capped per role
- **Role week note**: Rotational exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: High-eccentric tolerance allowed per role

- **Personalization**: Jump/landing volume capped per role

      Cue: Light feet, tall posture

      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (4 min)
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Band YTWs (1 min)
      Cue: Squeeze shoulder blades, keep ribs down
    - Pass-through (PVC) (0 min)
      Cue: Keep arms straight, go overhead and back
    - Dead bug (0 min)
      Cue: Press low back into floor, move opposite limbs
    - Cossack squat (1 min)
      Cue: Shift weight fully to bent leg, keep heel down

    - Sub-max squat (50-60%) (1 min)

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.FULL_BODY_STRENGTH — General strength accrual across all major movement patterns. Highest frequency of each pattern per week.
- **Best phase**: off_season, pre_season | **Best training age**: 0-3 years
- **Role week note**: Prop role -> maximal force / upper-body emphasis / collision robustness bias
- **Role week note**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: Jump/landing volume capped per role
- **Role week note**: Deceleration accumulation capped per role
- **Role week note**: Rotational exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: High-eccentric tolerance allowed per role
- **Personalization**: Prop role -> maximal force & collision robustness emphasis
- **Personalization**: Prop role -> maximal force / upper-body emphasis / collision robustness bias
- **Personalization**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Personalization**: Jump/landing volume capped per role
- **Rationale**: Blueprint: Full Body Strength (ID: 1)

### Why This Sample Exists

- Represents a **beginner Hooker** athlete in the **rugby** pathway.
- Training age of 0.5 year(s) with limited gym history.
- Goal emphasis: **Strength** — drives blueprint selection and session architecture.
- Competition context: **Off Season** — affects volume, intensity, and conditioning density.
- Equipment access: **Basic Gym** — constrains exercise selection and loading options.
- Variant **A** of two — differs in goal emphasis, training load, or seasonal context to show FORGE's range within the same archetype.

rugby Athlete (strength)
==================================================
Level: Beginner | 2x/week | 8 weeks

WARMUP
------------------------------
  Raise (3 min)
    - Forward jog (2 min)
      Cue: Light feet, tall posture
    - Skipping (basic) (1 min)
      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (4 min)
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Band YTWs (1 min)
      Cue: Squeeze shoulder blades, keep ribs down
    - Pass-through (PVC) (0 min)
      Cue: Keep arms straight, go overhead and back
    - Dead bug (0 min)
      Cue: Press low back into floor, move opposite limbs
    - Cossack squat (1 min)
      Cue: Shift weight fully to bent leg, keep heel down
  Potentiate (2 min)
    - Sub-max bench (50-60%) (1 min)
      Cue: Explosive concentric, controlled descent
    - Sub-max squat (50-60%) (1 min)
      Cue: Stand fast, controlled descent

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.YOUTH_FOUNDATION — Build movement literacy, general strength, and athleticism. No specialisation. Fun + competence.
- **Best phase**: off_season, pre_season, in_season, transition | **Best training age**: 0-2 years
- **Role week note**: Hooker role -> maximal force / collision robustness bias
- **Role week note**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: Deceleration accumulation capped per role
- **Role week note**: Rotational exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: High-eccentric tolerance allowed per role
- **Personalization**: Hooker role -> maximal force / collision robustness bias
- **Personalization**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Personalization**: Deceleration accumulation capped per role
- **Personalization**: Rotational exposure moderated per role
- **Rationale**: Blueprint: Youth Foundation (U14-U20) (ID: 7)
- **Rationale**: Goal: strength | Level: Beginner
- **Rationale**: Role-aware planning applied: hooker
- **Rationale**: Block: 8 weeks, 2x/week — 16 total sessions

- Represents a **beginner Hooker** athlete in the **rugby** pathway.
- Training age of 0.8 year(s) with limited gym history.
- Goal emphasis: **Power** — drives blueprint selection and session architecture.
- Competition context: **Pre Season** — affects volume, intensity, and conditioning density.
- Equipment access: **Basic Gym** — constrains exercise selection and loading options.
- Variant **B** of two — differs in goal emphasis, training load, or seasonal context to show FORGE's range within the same archetype.

rugby Athlete (power)
==================================================
Level: Beginner | 3x/week | 8 weeks

WARMUP
------------------------------
  Raise (3 min)
    - Forward jog (2 min)
      Cue: Light feet, tall posture
    - Skipping (basic) (1 min)
      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (3 min)
    - Leg swings (front/back) (1 min)
      Cue: Swing from hip, not lower back
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Pass-through (PVC) (0 min)
      Cue: Keep arms straight, go overhead and back
  Potentiate (2 min)
    - Pogo jumps (0 min)
      Cue: Minimal knee bend, stiff ankles, quick ground contact
    - Box jump (build-up) (1 min)
      Cue: Land soft, stick each rep
    - Band-resisted sprint starts (1 min)
      Cue: Drive hard for 5 steps

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.FULL_BODY_STRENGTH — General strength accrual across all major movement patterns. Highest frequency of each pattern per week.
- **Best phase**: off_season, pre_season | **Best training age**: 0-3 years
- **Role week note**: Hooker role -> maximal force / collision robustness bias
- **Role week note**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: Deceleration accumulation capped per role
- **Role week note**: Rotational exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: High-eccentric tolerance allowed per role
- **Personalization**: Hooker role -> maximal force / collision robustness bias
- **Personalization**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Personalization**: Deceleration accumulation capped per role
- **Personalization**: Rotational exposure moderated per role
- **Rationale**: Blueprint: Full Body Strength (ID: 1)
- **Rationale**: Goal: power | Level: Beginner
- **Rationale**: Role-aware planning applied: hooker
- **Rationale**: Block: 8 weeks, 3x/week — 24 total sessions

- Represents a **intermediate Hooker** athlete in the **rugby** pathway.
- Training age of 2.0 year(s) with solid gym history.
- Goal emphasis: **Strength** — drives blueprint selection and session architecture.
- Competition context: **Off Season** — affects volume, intensity, and conditioning density.
- Equipment access: **Commercial Gym** — constrains exercise selection and loading options.
- Variant **A** of two — differs in goal emphasis, training load, or seasonal context to show FORGE's range within the same archetype.

------------------------------
  Raise (3 min)
    - Forward jog (2 min)
      Cue: Light feet, tall posture
    - Skipping (basic) (1 min)
      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (4 min)
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Band YTWs (1 min)
      Cue: Squeeze shoulder blades, keep ribs down
    - Pass-through (PVC) (0 min)
      Cue: Keep arms straight, go overhead and back
    - Dead bug (0 min)
      Cue: Press low back into floor, move opposite limbs
    - Cossack squat (1 min)
      Cue: Shift weight fully to bent leg, keep heel down
  Potentiate (2 min)
    - Sub-max bench (50-60%) (1 min)
      Cue: Explosive concentric, controlled descent
    - Sub-max squat (50-60%) (1 min)
      Cue: Stand fast, controlled descent

rugby Athlete (power)
==================================================
Level: Intermediate | 3x/week | 8 weeks

WARMUP
------------------------------
  Raise (3 min)
    - Forward jog (2 min)
      Cue: Light feet, tall posture
    - Skipping (basic) (1 min)
      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (3 min)
    - Leg swings (front/back) (1 min)
      Cue: Swing from hip, not lower back
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Pass-through (PVC) (0 min)
      Cue: Keep arms straight, go overhead and back
  Potentiate (2 min)
    - Pogo jumps (0 min)
      Cue: Minimal knee bend, stiff ankles, quick ground contact
    - Box jump (build-up) (1 min)
      Cue: Land soft, stick each rep
    - Band-resisted sprint starts (1 min)
      Cue: Drive hard for 5 steps

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.FULL_BODY_STRENGTH — General strength accrual across all major movement patterns. Highest frequency of each pattern per week.
- **Best phase**: off_season, pre_season | **Best training age**: 0-3 years
- **Role week note**: Hooker role -> maximal force / collision robustness bias
- **Role week note**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: Deceleration accumulation capped per role
- **Role week note**: Rotational exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: High-eccentric tolerance allowed per role
- **Personalization**: Hooker role -> maximal force / collision robustness bias
- **Personalization**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Personalization**: Deceleration accumulation capped per role
- **Personalization**: Rotational exposure moderated per role
- **Rationale**: Blueprint: Full Body Strength (ID: 1)
- **Rationale**: Goal: power | Level: Intermediate
- **Rationale**: Role-aware planning applied: hooker
- **Rationale**: Block: 8 weeks, 3x/week — 24 total sessions

      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (4 min)
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Band YTWs (1 min)
      Cue: Squeeze shoulder blades, keep ribs down
    - Pass-through (PVC) (0 min)
      Cue: Keep arms straight, go overhead and back
    - Dead bug (0 min)
      Cue: Press low back into floor, move opposite limbs
    - Cossack squat (1 min)
      Cue: Shift weight fully to bent leg, keep heel down

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.FULL_BODY_STRENGTH — General strength accrual across all major movement patterns. Highest frequency of each pattern per week.
- **Best phase**: off_season, pre_season | **Best training age**: 0-3 years
- **Role week note**: Hooker role -> maximal force / collision robustness bias

- **Role week note**: Deceleration accumulation capped per role
- **Role week note**: Rotational exposure moderated per role

- **Personalization**: Rotational exposure moderated per role

  Raise (3 min)
    - Forward jog (2 min)
      Cue: Light feet, tall posture
    - Skipping (basic) (1 min)
      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (3 min)
    - Leg swings (front/back) (1 min)
      Cue: Swing from hip, not lower back
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Pass-through (PVC) (0 min)
      Cue: Keep arms straight, go overhead and back
  Potentiate (2 min)
    - Pogo jumps (0 min)
      Cue: Minimal knee bend, stiff ankles, quick ground contact
    - Box jump (build-up) (1 min)
      Cue: Land soft, stick each rep
    - Band-resisted sprint starts (1 min)

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.FULL_BODY_STRENGTH — General strength accrual across all major movement patterns. Highest frequency of each pattern per week.
- **Best phase**: off_season, pre_season | **Best training age**: 0-3 years
- **Role week note**: Hooker role -> maximal force / collision robustness bias
- **Role week note**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: Deceleration accumulation capped per role
- **Role week note**: Rotational exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: High-eccentric tolerance allowed per role
- **Personalization**: Hooker role -> maximal force / collision robustness bias
- **Personalization**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Personalization**: Deceleration accumulation capped per role
- **Personalization**: Rotational exposure moderated per role
- **Rationale**: Blueprint: Full Body Strength (ID: 1)
- **Rationale**: Goal: power | Level: Advanced
- **Rationale**: Role-aware planning applied: hooker
- **Rationale**: Block: 8 weeks, 3x/week — 24 total sessions

### Why This Sample Exists

- Represents a **beginner Lock** athlete in the **rugby** pathway.
- Training age of 0.5 year(s) with limited gym history.
- Goal emphasis: **Power** — drives blueprint selection and session architecture.
- Competition context: **Off Season** — affects volume, intensity, and conditioning density.
- Equipment access: **Basic Gym** — constrains exercise selection and loading options.
- Variant **A** of two — differs in goal emphasis, training load, or seasonal context to show FORGE's range within the same archetype.

```
rugby Athlete (power)
==================================================
Level: Beginner | 2x/week | 8 weeks
Goal: power | Equipment: Basic Gym

Personalization Notes:
  Lock role -> jump-landing & scrum force emphasis
  Lock role -> maximal force / collision robustness bias

WARMUP
------------------------------
  Raise (3 min)
    - Forward jog (2 min)
      Cue: Light feet, tall posture
    - Skipping (basic) (1 min)
      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (3 min)
    - Leg swings (front/back) (1 min)
      Cue: Swing from hip, not lower back
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Pass-through (PVC) (0 min)
      Cue: Keep arms straight, go overhead and back
  Potentiate (2 min)
    - Pogo jumps (0 min)
      Cue: Minimal knee bend, stiff ankles, quick ground contact
    - Box jump (build-up) (1 min)
      Cue: Land soft, stick each rep
    - Band-resisted sprint starts (1 min)
      Cue: Drive hard for 5 steps

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.YOUTH_FOUNDATION — Build movement literacy, general strength, and athleticism. No specialisation. Fun + competence.
- **Best phase**: off_season, pre_season, in_season, transition | **Best training age**: 0-2 years
- **Role week note**: Lock role -> maximal force / collision robustness bias
- **Role week note**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: Jump/landing volume elevated per role
- **Role week note**: Deceleration accumulation capped per role
- **Role week note**: Rotational exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: High-eccentric tolerance allowed per role
- **Personalization**: Lock role -> jump-landing & scrum force emphasis
- **Personalization**: Lock role -> maximal force / collision robustness bias
- **Personalization**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Personalization**: Jump/landing volume elevated per role
- **Rationale**: Blueprint: Youth Foundation (U14-U20) (ID: 7)
- **Rationale**: Goal: power | Level: Beginner
- **Rationale**: Role-aware planning applied: lock
- **Rationale**: Block: 8 weeks, 2x/week — 16 total sessions

### Coach Review Prompts

- [ ] Does this **Lock (Beginner)** program feel role-appropriate?
- [ ] Is the weekly training frequency appropriate for this athlete archetype?
- [ ] Are exercise selections and sequencing coach-like and sport-relevant?
- [ ] Does the conditioning density match the sport's physiological demands?
- [ ] Is the loading / intensity progression realistic for this level?
- [ ] What is the single most impactful change you would make?
- [ ] Would you trust this program with a real athlete at this level?

### Why This Sample Exists

- Represents a **beginner Lock** athlete in the **rugby** pathway.
- Training age of 0.8 year(s) with limited gym history.
- Goal emphasis: **Strength** — drives blueprint selection and session architecture.
- Competition context: **Pre Season** — affects volume, intensity, and conditioning density.
- Equipment access: **Basic Gym** — constrains exercise selection and loading options.
- Variant **B** of two — differs in goal emphasis, training load, or seasonal context to show FORGE's range within the same archetype.

------------------------------
  Raise (3 min)
    - Forward jog (2 min)
      Cue: Light feet, tall posture
    - Skipping (basic) (1 min)
      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (4 min)
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Band YTWs (1 min)
      Cue: Squeeze shoulder blades, keep ribs down
    - Pass-through (PVC) (0 min)
      Cue: Keep arms straight, go overhead and back
    - Dead bug (0 min)
      Cue: Press low back into floor, move opposite limbs
    - Cossack squat (1 min)
      Cue: Shift weight fully to bent leg, keep heel down
  Potentiate (2 min)
    - Sub-max bench (50-60%) (1 min)
      Cue: Explosive concentric, controlled descent
    - Sub-max squat (50-60%) (1 min)
      Cue: Stand fast, controlled descent

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.FULL_BODY_STRENGTH — General strength accrual across all major movement patterns. Highest frequency of each pattern per week.
- **Best phase**: off_season, pre_season | **Best training age**: 0-3 years
- **Role week note**: Lock role -> maximal force / collision robustness bias
- **Role week note**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: Jump/landing volume elevated per role
- **Role week note**: Deceleration accumulation capped per role
- **Role week note**: Rotational exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: High-eccentric tolerance allowed per role
- **Personalization**: Lock role -> jump-landing & scrum force emphasis
- **Personalization**: Lock role -> maximal force / collision robustness bias
- **Personalization**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Personalization**: Jump/landing volume elevated per role
- **Rationale**: Blueprint: Full Body Strength (ID: 1)
- **Rationale**: Goal: strength | Level: Beginner
- **Rationale**: Role-aware planning applied: lock
- **Rationale**: Block: 8 weeks, 3x/week — 24 total sessions

- [ ] Is the loading / intensity progression realistic for this level?
- [ ] What is the single most impactful change you would make?

### Why This Sample Exists

- Represents a **intermediate Lock** athlete in the **rugby** pathway.
- Training age of 2.0 year(s) with solid gym history.
- Goal emphasis: **Power** — drives blueprint selection and session architecture.
- Competition context: **Off Season** — affects volume, intensity, and conditioning density.
- Equipment access: **Commercial Gym** — constrains exercise selection and loading options.
- Variant **A** of two — differs in goal emphasis, training load, or seasonal context to show FORGE's range within the same archetype.

### Role Profile / Bias Summary

- Lock role -> maximal force / collision robustness bias
- Sprint exposure moderated per role
- Jump/landing volume elevated per role
- Deceleration accumulation capped per role
- Rotational exposure moderated per role
- High-eccentric tolerance allowed per role

  W4 (intensification     ): 20 ex | 4 sqt 0 hng 0 spd 0 lnd 0 rot
  W5 (intensification     ): 24 ex | 4 sqt 0 hng 0 spd 0 lnd 0 rot
  W6 (peak                ): 20 ex | 4 sqt 0 hng 0 spd 0 lnd 0 rot
  W7 (taper               ): 24 ex | 4 sqt 0 hng 0 spd 0 lnd 0 rot
  W8 (deload              ): 20 ex | 4 sqt 0 hng 0 spd 0 lnd 0 rot

  Role Exposure Targets:
    Sprint:        Low  | Jump/Landing: High
    Deceleration:  Low  | Rotation:     Low
    Conditioning:  Moderate

Role Week Bias:
  Lock role -> maximal force / collision robustness bias
  Sprint exposure moderated per role
  Jump/landing volume elevated per role
  Deceleration accumulation capped per role
  Rotational exposure moderated per role
  High-eccentric tolerance allowed per role

Role Weekly Emphasis Targets:
  Force emphasis:           0.8
  Velocity emphasis:        0.3
  Conditioning emphasis:    0.5
  Rotation emphasis:        0.4
  Landing emphasis:         0.6
  Upper-body emphasis:      0.6

  Sprint exposure target:   Low
  Jump/Landing exposure target: High
  Deceleration exposure target: Low
  Rotation exposure target: Low
  Conditioning density:     Moderate

Testing / Benchmarking:
  Week 1: Movement / Technical Benchmark, Jump / Power Test
  Week 5: Jump / Power Test, Sprint / Speed Test
  Week 6: Jump / Power Test, Sprint / Speed Test
  Week 8: Lower Body Strength Test, Upper Body Strength Test, Jump / Power Test, Sprint / Speed Test, Conditioning Benchmark

============================================================
```

### Full Program Output

```
rugby Athlete (power)
==================================================
Level: Intermediate | 4x/week | 8 weeks
Goal: power | Equipment: Commercial Gym

Personalization Notes:
  Lock role -> jump-landing & scrum force emphasis
  Lock role -> maximal force / collision robustness bias
  Sprint exposure moderated per role
  Jump/landing volume elevated per role
  Deceleration accumulation capped per role
  Rotational exposure moderated per role
  High-eccentric tolerance allowed per role

WARMUP
------------------------------
  Raise (3 min)
    - Forward jog (2 min)
      Cue: Light feet, tall posture
    - Skipping (basic) (1 min)
      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (3 min)
    - Leg swings (front/back) (1 min)
      Cue: Swing from hip, not lower back
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Pass-through (PVC) (0 min)
      Cue: Keep arms straight, go overhead and back
  Potentiate (2 min)
    - Pogo jumps (0 min)
      Cue: Minimal knee bend, stiff ankles, quick ground contact
    - Box jump (build-up) (1 min)
      Cue: Land soft, stick each rep
    - Band-resisted sprint starts (1 min)
      Cue: Drive hard for 5 steps

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.RUGBY_OFF_SEASON — Build collision-ready strength, power, and mass. Contact preparation with conditioning.
- **Best phase**: off_season | **Best training age**: 2-5 years
- **Role week note**: Lock role -> maximal force / collision robustness bias
- **Role week note**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: Jump/landing volume elevated per role
- **Role week note**: Deceleration accumulation capped per role
- **Role week note**: Rotational exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: High-eccentric tolerance allowed per role
- **Personalization**: Lock role -> jump-landing & scrum force emphasis
- **Personalization**: Lock role -> maximal force / collision robustness bias
- **Personalization**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Personalization**: Jump/landing volume elevated per role
- **Rationale**: Blueprint: Rugby Off-Season (ID: 9)
- **Rationale**: Goal: power | Level: Intermediate
- **Rationale**: Role-aware planning applied: lock
- **Rationale**: Block: 8 weeks, 4x/week — 32 total sessions

WARMUP
------------------------------
  Raise (3 min)
    - Forward jog (2 min)
      Cue: Light feet, tall posture
    - Skipping (basic) (1 min)
      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (4 min)
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Band YTWs (1 min)
      Cue: Squeeze shoulder blades, keep ribs down
    - Pass-through (PVC) (0 min)
      Cue: Keep arms straight, go overhead and back
    - Dead bug (0 min)
      Cue: Press low back into floor, move opposite limbs
    - Cossack squat (1 min)
      Cue: Shift weight fully to bent leg, keep heel down
  Potentiate (2 min)
    - Sub-max bench (50-60%) (1 min)
      Cue: Explosive concentric, controlled descent
    - Sub-max squat (50-60%) (1 min)
      Cue: Stand fast, controlled descent

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.FULL_BODY_STRENGTH — General strength accrual across all major movement patterns. Highest frequency of each pattern per week.
- **Best phase**: off_season, pre_season | **Best training age**: 0-3 years
- **Role week note**: Lock role -> maximal force / collision robustness bias
- **Role week note**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: Jump/landing volume elevated per role
- **Role week note**: Deceleration accumulation capped per role
- **Role week note**: Rotational exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: High-eccentric tolerance allowed per role
- **Personalization**: Lock role -> jump-landing & scrum force emphasis
- **Personalization**: Lock role -> maximal force / collision robustness bias
- **Personalization**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Personalization**: Jump/landing volume elevated per role
- **Rationale**: Blueprint: Full Body Strength (ID: 1)
- **Rationale**: Goal: strength | Level: Intermediate
- **Rationale**: Role-aware planning applied: lock
- **Rationale**: Block: 8 weeks, 3x/week — 24 total sessions

- Represents a **advanced Lock** athlete in the **rugby** pathway.
- Training age of 5.0 year(s) with extensive gym history.
- Goal emphasis: **Power** — drives blueprint selection and session architecture.
- Competition context: **Pre Season** — affects volume, intensity, and conditioning density.
- Equipment access: **Commercial Gym** — constrains exercise selection and loading options.
- Variant **A** of two — differs in goal emphasis, training load, or seasonal context to show FORGE's range within the same archetype.

------------------------------
  Raise (3 min)
    - Forward jog (2 min)
      Cue: Light feet, tall posture
    - Skipping (basic) (1 min)
      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (3 min)
    - Leg swings (front/back) (1 min)
      Cue: Swing from hip, not lower back
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Pass-through (PVC) (0 min)
      Cue: Keep arms straight, go overhead and back
  Potentiate (2 min)
    - Pogo jumps (0 min)
      Cue: Minimal knee bend, stiff ankles, quick ground contact
    - Box jump (build-up) (1 min)
      Cue: Land soft, stick each rep
    - Band-resisted sprint starts (1 min)
      Cue: Drive hard for 5 steps

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.FULL_BODY_STRENGTH — General strength accrual across all major movement patterns. Highest frequency of each pattern per week.
- **Best phase**: off_season, pre_season | **Best training age**: 0-3 years
- **Role week note**: Lock role -> maximal force / collision robustness bias
- **Role week note**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: Jump/landing volume elevated per role
- **Role week note**: Deceleration accumulation capped per role
- **Role week note**: Rotational exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: High-eccentric tolerance allowed per role
- **Personalization**: Lock role -> jump-landing & scrum force emphasis
- **Personalization**: Lock role -> maximal force / collision robustness bias
- **Personalization**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Personalization**: Jump/landing volume elevated per role

- **Rationale**: Goal: power | Level: Advanced

- **Rationale**: Block: 8 weeks, 3x/week — 24 total sessions

  Raise (3 min)
    - Forward jog (2 min)
      Cue: Light feet, tall posture
    - Skipping (basic) (1 min)
      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (4 min)
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Band YTWs (1 min)
      Cue: Squeeze shoulder blades, keep ribs down
    - Pass-through (PVC) (0 min)
      Cue: Keep arms straight, go overhead and back
    - Dead bug (0 min)
      Cue: Press low back into floor, move opposite limbs
    - Cossack squat (1 min)
      Cue: Shift weight fully to bent leg, keep heel down
  Potentiate (2 min)
    - Sub-max bench (50-60%) (1 min)
      Cue: Explosive concentric, controlled descent
    - Sub-max squat (50-60%) (1 min)
      Cue: Stand fast, controlled descent

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.FULL_BODY_STRENGTH — General strength accrual across all major movement patterns. Highest frequency of each pattern per week.
- **Best phase**: off_season, pre_season | **Best training age**: 0-3 years
- **Role week note**: Lock role -> maximal force / collision robustness bias
- **Role week note**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: Jump/landing volume elevated per role
- **Role week note**: Deceleration accumulation capped per role
- **Role week note**: Rotational exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: High-eccentric tolerance allowed per role
- **Personalization**: Lock role -> jump-landing & scrum force emphasis
- **Personalization**: Lock role -> maximal force / collision robustness bias
- **Personalization**: Sprint exposure moderated per role
- **Personalization**: Jump/landing volume elevated per role
- **Rationale**: Blueprint: Full Body Strength (ID: 1)
- **Rationale**: Goal: strength | Level: Advanced
- **Rationale**: Role-aware planning applied: lock
- **Rationale**: Block: 8 weeks, 3x/week — 24 total sessions

rugby Athlete (conditioning)
==================================================
Level: Beginner | 2x/week | 8 weeks
Goal: conditioning | Equipment: Basic Gym

Personalization Notes:
  Back-row role -> hybrid force & repeat-running emphasis
  Back Row role -> repeat conditioning / collision robustness bias

WARMUP
------------------------------
  Raise (3 min)
    - Forward jog (2 min)
      Cue: Light feet, tall posture
    - Skipping (basic) (1 min)
      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (3 min)
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Dead bug (0 min)
      Cue: Press low back into floor, move opposite limbs
    - Cossack squat (1 min)
      Cue: Shift weight fully to bent leg, keep heel down
  Potentiate (1 min)
    - Pogo jumps (0 min)
      Cue: Minimal knee bend, stiff ankles, quick ground contact
    - Build-up sprint (60-80%) (1 min)
      Cue: Progressive acceleration, tall posture

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.YOUTH_FOUNDATION — Build movement literacy, general strength, and athleticism. No specialisation. Fun + competence.
- **Best phase**: off_season, pre_season, in_season, transition | **Best training age**: 0-2 years
- **Role week note**: Back Row role -> repeat conditioning / collision robustness bias
- **Role week note**: Rotational exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: Higher conditioning density than role baseline
- **Role week note**: High-eccentric tolerance allowed per role
- **Personalization**: Back-row role -> hybrid force & repeat-running emphasis
- **Personalization**: Back Row role -> repeat conditioning / collision robustness bias
- **Personalization**: Rotational exposure moderated per role
- **Personalization**: Higher conditioning density than role baseline
- **Rationale**: Blueprint: Youth Foundation (U14-U20) (ID: 7)
- **Rationale**: Goal: conditioning | Level: Beginner
- **Rationale**: Role-aware planning applied: back_row
- **Rationale**: Block: 8 weeks, 2x/week — 16 total sessions

### Why This Sample Exists

- Represents a **beginner Back Row** athlete in the **rugby** pathway.
- Training age of 0.8 year(s) with limited gym history.
- Goal emphasis: **Power** — drives blueprint selection and session architecture.
- Competition context: **Pre Season** — affects volume, intensity, and conditioning density.
- Equipment access: **Basic Gym** — constrains exercise selection and loading options.
- Variant **B** of two — differs in goal emphasis, training load, or seasonal context to show FORGE's range within the same archetype.

### Role Profile / Bias Summary

- Back Row role -> repeat conditioning / collision robustness bias
- Rotational exposure moderated per role
- Higher conditioning density than role baseline
- High-eccentric tolerance allowed per role

-   Force emphasis:           0.6
-   Velocity emphasis:        0.5
-   Conditioning emphasis:    0.7
-   Rotation emphasis:        0.4
-   Landing emphasis:         0.5
-   Upper-body emphasis:      0.5
- 
-   Sprint exposure target:   Moderate
-   Jump/Landing exposure target: Moderate
-   Deceleration exposure target: Moderate
-   Rotation exposure target: Low
-   Conditioning density:     High

WARMUP
------------------------------
  Raise (3 min)
    - Forward jog (2 min)
      Cue: Light feet, tall posture
    - Skipping (basic) (1 min)
      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (3 min)
    - Leg swings (front/back) (1 min)
      Cue: Swing from hip, not lower back
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Pass-through (PVC) (0 min)
      Cue: Keep arms straight, go overhead and back
  Potentiate (2 min)
    - Pogo jumps (0 min)
      Cue: Minimal knee bend, stiff ankles, quick ground contact
    - Box jump (build-up) (1 min)
      Cue: Land soft, stick each rep
    - Band-resisted sprint starts (1 min)
      Cue: Drive hard for 5 steps

    - Scapular Retraction  1-2 x 10-15  |  pain-free, quality  |  rest: 45s

  [Core] Core
    - Plank  1-2 x 20-30s holds / 8-12 reps  |  quality, brace  |  rest: 45s
  Est. time: 30 min

RECOVERY
------------------------------
  L1: Mobility + stretch (L1) — 10 min
  - General mobility flow (10 min)

==================================================

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.FULL_BODY_STRENGTH — General strength accrual across all major movement patterns. Highest frequency of each pattern per week.
- **Best phase**: off_season, pre_season | **Best training age**: 0-3 years
- **Role week note**: Back Row role -> repeat conditioning / collision robustness bias
- **Role week note**: Rotational exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: Higher conditioning density than role baseline
- **Role week note**: High-eccentric tolerance allowed per role

      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground

    - Dead bug (0 min)

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.RUGBY_OFF_SEASON — Build collision-ready strength, power, and mass. Contact preparation with conditioning.
- **Best phase**: off_season | **Best training age**: 2-5 years
- **Role week note**: Back Row role -> repeat conditioning / collision robustness bias
- **Role week note**: Rotational exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: Higher conditioning density than role baseline
- **Role week note**: High-eccentric tolerance allowed per role
- **Personalization**: Back-row role -> hybrid force & repeat-running emphasis
- **Personalization**: Back Row role -> repeat conditioning / collision robustness bias
- **Personalization**: Rotational exposure moderated per role
- **Personalization**: Higher conditioning density than role baseline

- **Rationale**: Role-aware planning applied: back_row

- Equipment access: **Commercial Gym** — constrains exercise selection and loading options.
- Variant **B** of two — differs in goal emphasis, training load, or seasonal context to show FORGE's range within the same archetype.

rugby Athlete (power)
==================================================
Level: Intermediate | 3x/week | 8 weeks
Goal: power | Equipment: Commercial Gym

Personalization Notes:
  Back-row role -> hybrid force & repeat-running emphasis
  Back Row role -> repeat conditioning / collision robustness bias

WARMUP
------------------------------
  Raise (3 min)
    - Forward jog (2 min)
      Cue: Light feet, tall posture
    - Skipping (basic) (1 min)
      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (3 min)
    - Leg swings (front/back) (1 min)
      Cue: Swing from hip, not lower back
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Pass-through (PVC) (0 min)
      Cue: Keep arms straight, go overhead and back
  Potentiate (2 min)
    - Pogo jumps (0 min)
      Cue: Minimal knee bend, stiff ankles, quick ground contact
    - Box jump (build-up) (1 min)
      Cue: Land soft, stick each rep
    - Band-resisted sprint starts (1 min)
      Cue: Drive hard for 5 steps

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.FULL_BODY_STRENGTH — General strength accrual across all major movement patterns. Highest frequency of each pattern per week.
- **Best phase**: off_season, pre_season | **Best training age**: 0-3 years
- **Role week note**: Back Row role -> repeat conditioning / collision robustness bias
- **Role week note**: Rotational exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: Higher conditioning density than role baseline
- **Role week note**: High-eccentric tolerance allowed per role
- **Personalization**: Back-row role -> hybrid force & repeat-running emphasis
- **Personalization**: Back Row role -> repeat conditioning / collision robustness bias
- **Personalization**: Rotational exposure moderated per role
- **Personalization**: Higher conditioning density than role baseline
- **Rationale**: Blueprint: Full Body Strength (ID: 1)
- **Rationale**: Goal: power | Level: Intermediate
- **Rationale**: Role-aware planning applied: back_row
- **Rationale**: Block: 8 weeks, 3x/week — 24 total sessions

- Equipment access: **Commercial Gym** — constrains exercise selection and loading options.
- Variant **A** of two — differs in goal emphasis, training load, or seasonal context to show FORGE's range within the same archetype.

WARMUP
------------------------------
  Raise (3 min)
    - Forward jog (2 min)
      Cue: Light feet, tall posture
    - Skipping (basic) (1 min)
      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (3 min)
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Dead bug (0 min)
      Cue: Press low back into floor, move opposite limbs
    - Cossack squat (1 min)
      Cue: Shift weight fully to bent leg, keep heel down
  Potentiate (1 min)
    - Pogo jumps (0 min)
      Cue: Minimal knee bend, stiff ankles, quick ground contact
    - Build-up sprint (60-80%) (1 min)
      Cue: Progressive acceleration, tall posture

------------------------------
  Raise (3 min)
    - Forward jog (2 min)
      Cue: Light feet, tall posture
    - Skipping (basic) (1 min)
      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (3 min)
    - Leg swings (front/back) (1 min)
      Cue: Swing from hip, not lower back
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Pass-through (PVC) (0 min)
      Cue: Keep arms straight, go overhead and back
  Potentiate (2 min)
    - Pogo jumps (0 min)
      Cue: Minimal knee bend, stiff ankles, quick ground contact
    - Box jump (build-up) (1 min)
      Cue: Land soft, stick each rep
    - Band-resisted sprint starts (1 min)
      Cue: Drive hard for 5 steps

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.FULL_BODY_STRENGTH — General strength accrual across all major movement patterns. Highest frequency of each pattern per week.
- **Best phase**: off_season, pre_season | **Best training age**: 0-3 years
- **Role week note**: Back Row role -> repeat conditioning / collision robustness bias
- **Role week note**: Rotational exposure moderated per role
- **Role week note**: Higher conditioning density than role baseline
- **Role week note**: High-eccentric tolerance allowed per role
- **Personalization**: Back-row role -> hybrid force & repeat-running emphasis
- **Personalization**: Back Row role -> repeat conditioning / collision robustness bias
- **Personalization**: Rotational exposure moderated per role
- **Personalization**: Higher conditioning density than role baseline

### Why This Sample Exists

- Represents a **beginner Scrum Half** athlete in the **rugby** pathway.
- Training age of 0.5 year(s) with limited gym history.
- Goal emphasis: **Speed** — drives blueprint selection and session architecture.
- Competition context: **Off Season** — affects volume, intensity, and conditioning density.
- Equipment access: **Basic Gym** — constrains exercise selection and loading options.
- Variant **A** of two — differs in goal emphasis, training load, or seasonal context to show FORGE's range within the same archetype.

- Scrum Half role -> velocity / speed-power bias

- Jump/landing volume capped per role

      Cue: Light feet, tall posture
    - Skipping (basic) (1 min)
      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (3 min)
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Dead bug (0 min)
      Cue: Press low back into floor, move opposite limbs
    - Cossack squat (1 min)
      Cue: Shift weight fully to bent leg, keep heel down
  Potentiate (1 min)
    - Pogo jumps (0 min)
      Cue: Minimal knee bend, stiff ankles, quick ground contact
    - Build-up sprint (60-80%) (1 min)

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.YOUTH_FOUNDATION — Build movement literacy, general strength, and athleticism. No specialisation. Fun + competence.
- **Best phase**: off_season, pre_season, in_season, transition | **Best training age**: 0-2 years
- **Role week note**: Scrum Half role -> velocity / speed-power bias
- **Role week note**: Sprint exposure elevated per role
- **Role week note**: Jump/landing volume capped per role
- **Role week note**: Higher conditioning density than role baseline
- **Personalization**: Scrum Half role -> velocity / speed-power bias
- **Personalization**: Sprint exposure elevated per role
- **Personalization**: Jump/landing volume capped per role
- **Personalization**: Higher conditioning density than role baseline
- **Rationale**: Blueprint: Youth Foundation (U14-U20) (ID: 7)
- **Rationale**: Goal: speed | Level: Beginner
- **Rationale**: Role-aware planning applied: scrum_half
- **Rationale**: Block: 8 weeks, 2x/week — 16 total sessions

### Why This Sample Exists

- Represents a **beginner Scrum Half** athlete in the **rugby** pathway.
- Training age of 0.8 year(s) with limited gym history.
- Goal emphasis: **Conditioning** — drives blueprint selection and session architecture.
- Competition context: **Pre Season** — affects volume, intensity, and conditioning density.
- Equipment access: **Basic Gym** — constrains exercise selection and loading options.
- Variant **B** of two — differs in goal emphasis, training load, or seasonal context to show FORGE's range within the same archetype.

rugby Athlete (conditioning)
==================================================
Level: Beginner | 3x/week | 8 weeks
Goal: conditioning | Equipment: Basic Gym

  Scrum Half role -> velocity / speed-power bias
  Sprint exposure elevated per role
  Jump/landing volume capped per role

WARMUP
------------------------------
  Raise (3 min)
    - Forward jog (2 min)
      Cue: Light feet, tall posture
    - Skipping (basic) (1 min)
      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (3 min)
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Dead bug (0 min)
      Cue: Press low back into floor, move opposite limbs
    - Cossack squat (1 min)
      Cue: Shift weight fully to bent leg, keep heel down
  Potentiate (1 min)
    - Pogo jumps (0 min)
      Cue: Minimal knee bend, stiff ankles, quick ground contact
    - Build-up sprint (60-80%) (1 min)
      Cue: Progressive acceleration, tall posture

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.STRENGTH_CONDITIONING — Strength maintenance + energy system development. Common in pre-season / GPP blocks.
- **Best phase**: pre_season, off_season | **Best training age**: 1-4 years
- **Role week note**: Scrum Half role -> velocity / speed-power bias
- **Role week note**: Sprint exposure elevated per role
- **Role week note**: Jump/landing volume capped per role
- **Role week note**: Higher conditioning density than role baseline
- **Personalization**: Scrum Half role -> velocity / speed-power bias
- **Personalization**: Sprint exposure elevated per role
- **Personalization**: Jump/landing volume capped per role
- **Personalization**: Higher conditioning density than role baseline
- **Rationale**: Blueprint: Strength + Conditioning (ID: 3)
- **Rationale**: Goal: conditioning | Level: Beginner
- **Rationale**: Role-aware planning applied: scrum_half
- **Rationale**: Block: 8 weeks, 3x/week — 24 total sessions

### Why This Sample Exists

- Represents a **intermediate Scrum Half** athlete in the **rugby** pathway.
- Training age of 2.0 year(s) with solid gym history.
- Goal emphasis: **Speed** — drives blueprint selection and session architecture.
- Competition context: **Off Season** — affects volume, intensity, and conditioning density.
- Equipment access: **Commercial Gym** — constrains exercise selection and loading options.
- Variant **A** of two — differs in goal emphasis, training load, or seasonal context to show FORGE's range within the same archetype.

  Raise (3 min)

      Cue: Light feet, tall posture
    - Skipping (basic) (1 min)
      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (3 min)
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Dead bug (0 min)
      Cue: Press low back into floor, move opposite limbs
    - Cossack squat (1 min)
      Cue: Shift weight fully to bent leg, keep heel down
  Potentiate (1 min)
    - Pogo jumps (0 min)
      Cue: Minimal knee bend, stiff ankles, quick ground contact
    - Build-up sprint (60-80%) (1 min)

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.RUGBY_OFF_SEASON — Build collision-ready strength, power, and mass. Contact preparation with conditioning.
- **Best phase**: off_season | **Best training age**: 2-5 years
- **Role week note**: Scrum Half role -> velocity / speed-power bias
- **Role week note**: Sprint exposure elevated per role
- **Role week note**: Jump/landing volume capped per role
- **Role week note**: Higher conditioning density than role baseline
- **Personalization**: Scrum Half role -> velocity / speed-power bias
- **Personalization**: Sprint exposure elevated per role
- **Personalization**: Jump/landing volume capped per role
- **Personalization**: Higher conditioning density than role baseline
- **Rationale**: Blueprint: Rugby Off-Season (ID: 9)
- **Rationale**: Goal: speed | Level: Intermediate
- **Rationale**: Role-aware planning applied: scrum_half
- **Rationale**: Block: 8 weeks, 4x/week — 32 total sessions

### Why This Sample Exists

- Represents a **intermediate Scrum Half** athlete in the **rugby** pathway.
- Training age of 2.5 year(s) with solid gym history.
- Goal emphasis: **Conditioning** — drives blueprint selection and session architecture.
- Competition context: **Pre Season** — affects volume, intensity, and conditioning density.
- Equipment access: **Commercial Gym** — constrains exercise selection and loading options.
- Variant **B** of two — differs in goal emphasis, training load, or seasonal context to show FORGE's range within the same archetype.

- Scrum Half role -> velocity / speed-power bias
- Sprint exposure elevated per role
- Jump/landing volume capped per role
- Higher conditioning density than role baseline

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|

| **Duration** | 8 weeks |
| **Frequency** | 3x/week |
| **Conditioning Emphasis** | Pre Season — Conditioning |
| **Competition Window** | Off-season |
| **Role Emphasis** | scrum_half — rugby |
| **Credibility Score** | 1.0 |

rugby Athlete (conditioning)

Testing / Benchmarking:

### Full Program Output

```
rugby Athlete (conditioning)
==================================================
Level: Intermediate | 3x/week | 8 weeks
Goal: conditioning | Equipment: Commercial Gym

Personalization Notes:
  Scrum Half role -> velocity / speed-power bias
  Sprint exposure elevated per role
  Jump/landing volume capped per role
  Higher conditioning density than role baseline

WARMUP
------------------------------
  Raise (3 min)
    - Forward jog (2 min)
      Cue: Light feet, tall posture
    - Skipping (basic) (1 min)
      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (3 min)
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Dead bug (0 min)
      Cue: Press low back into floor, move opposite limbs
    - Cossack squat (1 min)
      Cue: Shift weight fully to bent leg, keep heel down
  Potentiate (1 min)
    - Pogo jumps (0 min)
      Cue: Minimal knee bend, stiff ankles, quick ground contact
    - Build-up sprint (60-80%) (1 min)
      Cue: Progressive acceleration, tall posture

    - Acceleration Wall Drill  3-4 x 20-30m  |  build to max  |  rest: 2:30 min

    60m in target time (determined by MAS test: distance covered in 60s all-out = MAS pace per 60m). Typically 8-10s per 60m.
  Est. time: 40 min

--- W8 S24 (test) ---
  [TEST] Lower Body Strength Test | Upper Body Strength Test | Jump / Power Test | Sprint / Speed Test | Conditioning Benchmark
  [Sprint] Sprint
    - Acceleration Wall Drill  3-4 x 20-30m  |  build to max  |  rest: 2:30 min
  [DLKD] DLKD
    - Goblet Squat  2 x 6-8  |  RPE 7-9  |  rest: 4:00 min
  [DLHD] DLHD
    - RDL  2 x 6-8  |  RPE 7-9  |  rest: 4:00 min
  [HPush] HPush
    - Barbell Bench Press  2 x 8-10  |  RPE 7-8  |  rest: 90s
  [HPull] HPull
    - Dumbbell Row  2 x 8-10  |  RPE 7-8  |  rest: 90s
  [Core] Core
    - Plank  2 x 30-45s holds / 10-15 reps  |  quality, anti-movement  |  rest: 45s
  Est. time: 30 min

RECOVERY
------------------------------
  L1: Mobility + stretch (L1) — 10 min
  - General mobility flow (10 min)

==================================================

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.STRENGTH_CONDITIONING — Strength maintenance + energy system development. Common in pre-season / GPP blocks.
- **Best phase**: pre_season, off_season | **Best training age**: 1-4 years
- **Role week note**: Scrum Half role -> velocity / speed-power bias
- **Role week note**: Sprint exposure elevated per role
- **Role week note**: Jump/landing volume capped per role
- **Role week note**: Higher conditioning density than role baseline
- **Personalization**: Scrum Half role -> velocity / speed-power bias
- **Personalization**: Sprint exposure elevated per role
- **Personalization**: Jump/landing volume capped per role
- **Personalization**: Higher conditioning density than role baseline
- **Rationale**: Blueprint: Strength + Conditioning (ID: 3)
- **Rationale**: Goal: conditioning | Level: Intermediate
- **Rationale**: Role-aware planning applied: scrum_half
- **Rationale**: Block: 8 weeks, 3x/week — 24 total sessions

    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Dead bug (0 min)

    - Cossack squat (1 min)
      Cue: Shift weight fully to bent leg, keep heel down

    - Pogo jumps (0 min)

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.SPRINT_DEVELOPMENT — Max velocity and/or acceleration improvement. Strength underpins but speed is priority.
- **Best phase**: pre_season, off_season, in_season | **Best training age**: 2-5 years
- **Role week note**: Scrum Half role -> velocity / speed-power bias
- **Role week note**: Sprint exposure elevated per role
- **Role week note**: Jump/landing volume capped per role

    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Dead bug (0 min)
      Cue: Press low back into floor, move opposite limbs
    - Cossack squat (1 min)
      Cue: Shift weight fully to bent leg, keep heel down

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.FULL_BODY_STRENGTH — General strength accrual across all major movement patterns. Highest frequency of each pattern per week.
- **Best phase**: off_season, pre_season | **Best training age**: 0-3 years
- **Role week note**: Scrum Half role -> velocity / speed-power bias
- **Role week note**: Sprint exposure elevated per role
- **Role week note**: Jump/landing volume capped per role

- Represents a **beginner Fly Half** athlete in the **rugby** pathway.
- Training age of 0.5 year(s) with limited gym history.
- Goal emphasis: **Power** — drives blueprint selection and session architecture.
- Competition context: **Off Season** — affects volume, intensity, and conditioning density.
- Equipment access: **Basic Gym** — constrains exercise selection and loading options.
- Variant **A** of two — differs in goal emphasis, training load, or seasonal context to show FORGE's range within the same archetype.

rugby Athlete (power)

WARMUP
------------------------------
  Raise (3 min)
    - Forward jog (2 min)
      Cue: Light feet, tall posture
    - Skipping (basic) (1 min)
      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (3 min)
    - Leg swings (front/back) (1 min)
      Cue: Swing from hip, not lower back
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Pass-through (PVC) (0 min)
      Cue: Keep arms straight, go overhead and back
  Potentiate (2 min)
    - Pogo jumps (0 min)
      Cue: Minimal knee bend, stiff ankles, quick ground contact
    - Box jump (build-up) (1 min)
      Cue: Land soft, stick each rep
    - Band-resisted sprint starts (1 min)
      Cue: Drive hard for 5 steps

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.YOUTH_FOUNDATION — Build movement literacy, general strength, and athleticism. No specialisation. Fun + competence.
- **Best phase**: off_season, pre_season, in_season, transition | **Best training age**: 0-2 years
- **Role week note**: Fly Half role -> balanced week emphasis
- **Role week note**: Jump/landing volume capped per role
- **Role week note**: Rotational exposure elevated per role
- **Role week note**: Higher conditioning density than role baseline
- **Personalization**: Fly Half role -> balanced week emphasis
- **Personalization**: Jump/landing volume capped per role
- **Personalization**: Rotational exposure elevated per role
- **Personalization**: Higher conditioning density than role baseline
- **Rationale**: Blueprint: Youth Foundation (U14-U20) (ID: 7)
- **Rationale**: Goal: power | Level: Beginner
- **Rationale**: Role-aware planning applied: fly_half
- **Rationale**: Block: 8 weeks, 2x/week — 16 total sessions

- Represents a **beginner Fly Half** athlete in the **rugby** pathway.

- Competition context: **Pre Season** — affects volume, intensity, and conditioning density.
- Equipment access: **Basic Gym** — constrains exercise selection and loading options.
- Variant **B** of two — differs in goal emphasis, training load, or seasonal context to show FORGE's range within the same archetype.

WARMUP
------------------------------
  Raise (3 min)
    - Forward jog (2 min)
      Cue: Light feet, tall posture
    - Skipping (basic) (1 min)
      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (3 min)
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Dead bug (0 min)
      Cue: Press low back into floor, move opposite limbs
    - Cossack squat (1 min)
      Cue: Shift weight fully to bent leg, keep heel down
  Potentiate (1 min)
    - Pogo jumps (0 min)
      Cue: Minimal knee bend, stiff ankles, quick ground contact
    - Build-up sprint (60-80%) (1 min)
      Cue: Progressive acceleration, tall posture

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.SPRINT_DEVELOPMENT — Max velocity and/or acceleration improvement. Strength underpins but speed is priority.
- **Best phase**: pre_season, off_season, in_season | **Best training age**: 2-5 years
- **Role week note**: Fly Half role -> balanced week emphasis
- **Role week note**: Jump/landing volume capped per role
- **Role week note**: Rotational exposure elevated per role
- **Role week note**: Higher conditioning density than role baseline
- **Personalization**: Fly Half role -> balanced week emphasis
- **Personalization**: Jump/landing volume capped per role
- **Personalization**: Rotational exposure elevated per role
- **Personalization**: Higher conditioning density than role baseline
- **Rationale**: Blueprint: Sprint Development (ID: 10)
- **Rationale**: Goal: speed | Level: Beginner
- **Rationale**: Role-aware planning applied: fly_half
- **Rationale**: Block: 8 weeks, 3x/week — 24 total sessions

- Represents a **intermediate Fly Half** athlete in the **rugby** pathway.
- Training age of 2.0 year(s) with solid gym history.

- Competition context: **Off Season** — affects volume, intensity, and conditioning density.
- Equipment access: **Commercial Gym** — constrains exercise selection and loading options.
- Variant **A** of two — differs in goal emphasis, training load, or seasonal context to show FORGE's range within the same archetype.

rugby Athlete (power)
==================================================
Level: Intermediate | 4x/week | 8 weeks

WARMUP
------------------------------
  Raise (3 min)
    - Forward jog (2 min)
      Cue: Light feet, tall posture
    - Skipping (basic) (1 min)
      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (3 min)
    - Leg swings (front/back) (1 min)
      Cue: Swing from hip, not lower back
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Pass-through (PVC) (0 min)
      Cue: Keep arms straight, go overhead and back
  Potentiate (2 min)
    - Pogo jumps (0 min)
      Cue: Minimal knee bend, stiff ankles, quick ground contact
    - Box jump (build-up) (1 min)
      Cue: Land soft, stick each rep
    - Band-resisted sprint starts (1 min)
      Cue: Drive hard for 5 steps

RECOVERY
------------------------------
  L1: Mobility + stretch (L1) — 10 min

      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Dead bug (0 min)
      Cue: Press low back into floor, move opposite limbs
    - Cossack squat (1 min)

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.SPRINT_DEVELOPMENT — Max velocity and/or acceleration improvement. Strength underpins but speed is priority.
- **Best phase**: pre_season, off_season, in_season | **Best training age**: 2-5 years
- **Role week note**: Fly Half role -> balanced week emphasis
- **Role week note**: Jump/landing volume capped per role
- **Role week note**: Rotational exposure elevated per role
- **Role week note**: Higher conditioning density than role baseline
- **Personalization**: Fly Half role -> balanced week emphasis
- **Personalization**: Jump/landing volume capped per role
- **Personalization**: Rotational exposure elevated per role
- **Personalization**: Higher conditioning density than role baseline
- **Rationale**: Blueprint: Sprint Development (ID: 10)
- **Rationale**: Goal: speed | Level: Intermediate
- **Rationale**: Role-aware planning applied: fly_half
- **Rationale**: Block: 8 weeks, 3x/week — 24 total sessions

  Raise (3 min)

      Cue: Light feet, tall posture
    - Skipping (basic) (1 min)
      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (3 min)
    - Leg swings (front/back) (1 min)
      Cue: Swing from hip, not lower back
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Pass-through (PVC) (0 min)
      Cue: Keep arms straight, go overhead and back
  Potentiate (2 min)
    - Pogo jumps (0 min)
      Cue: Minimal knee bend, stiff ankles, quick ground contact
    - Box jump (build-up) (1 min)
      Cue: Land soft, stick each rep
    - Band-resisted sprint starts (1 min)
      Cue: Drive hard for 5 steps

### Program Rationale

- **Blueprint**: BlueprintName.FULL_BODY_STRENGTH — General strength accrual across all major movement patterns. Highest frequency of each pattern per week.
- **Best phase**: off_season, pre_season | **Best training age**: 0-3 years
- **Role week note**: Fly Half role -> balanced week emphasis
- **Role week note**: Jump/landing volume capped per role
- **Role week note**: Rotational exposure elevated per role
- **Role week note**: Higher conditioning density than role baseline
- **Personalization**: Fly Half role -> balanced week emphasis
- **Personalization**: Jump/landing volume capped per role
- **Personalization**: Rotational exposure elevated per role
- **Personalization**: Higher conditioning density than role baseline

### Why This Sample Exists

- Represents a **beginner Centre** athlete in the **rugby** pathway.
- Training age of 0.5 year(s) with limited gym history.
- Goal emphasis: **Power** — drives blueprint selection and session architecture.
- Competition context: **Off Season** — affects volume, intensity, and conditioning density.
- Equipment access: **Basic Gym** — constrains exercise selection and loading options.
- Variant **A** of two — differs in goal emphasis, training load, or seasonal context to show FORGE's range within the same archetype.

```
rugby Athlete (power)
==================================================
Level: Beginner | 2x/week | 8 weeks
Goal: power | Equipment: Basic Gym

Personalization Notes:
  Centre role -> collision robustness bias
  Jump/landing volume capped per role
  Deceleration emphasis elevated per role

WARMUP
------------------------------
  Raise (3 min)
    - Forward jog (2 min)
      Cue: Light feet, tall posture
    - Skipping (basic) (1 min)
      Cue: Soft feet, quick ground contact
  Activate (3 min)
    - Leg swings (front/back) (1 min)
      Cue: Swing from hip, not lower back
    - World's greatest stretch (1 min)
      Cue: Open chest to ceiling, back knee off ground
    - Glute bridge (double) (0 min)
      Cue: Squeeze glutes at top, 2s hold
    - Pass-through (PVC) (0 min)
      Cue: Keep arms straight, go overhead and back
  Potentiate (2 min)
    - Pogo jumps (0 min)
      Cue: Minimal knee bend, stiff ankles, quick ground contact
    - Box jump (build-up) (1 min)
      Cue: Land soft, stick each rep
    - Band-resisted sprint starts (1 min)
      Cue: Drive hard for 5 steps

--- W1 S1 (accumulation) ---
  [TEST] Movement / Technical Benchmark | Jump / Power Test
  [Sprint] Sprint
    - Acceleration Wall Drill  3 x 15-20m  |  technique focus  |  rest: 2:00 min
  [DLKD] DLKD
    - Air Squat  3 x 8-12  |  RPE 7-8  |  rest: 3:00 min
  [DLHD] DLHD
